chapter_title: Registration of Off-Highway Vehicles; Original and Renewal of Identification; Issuance of Certificates of Ownership
text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 38020, an unidentified off-highway motor vehicle subject to identification may be left standing upon a highway or public or private property adjacent to the place of business of a dealer of such motor vehicles when done so in connection with the loading and unloading or storage of such vehicles to be used in the dealer’s business, unless already prohibited by law.
markdown:
# Vehicle Code
## Division 16.5. OFF-HIGHWAY VEHICLES [38000 - 38604]
### Chapter 2. Registration of Off-Highway Vehicles; Original and Renewal of Identification; Issuance of Certificates of Ownership [38010 - 38265]
#### Article 1. Motor Vehicles Subject to Identification [38010 - 38030]
##### § 38030

Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 38020, an unidentified off-highway motor vehicle subject to identification may be left standing upon a highway or public or private property adjacent to the place of business of a dealer of such motor vehicles when done so in connection with the loading and unloading or storage of such vehicles to be used in the dealer’s business, unless already prohibited by law.

*(Amended by Stats. 1973, Ch. 78.)*

subsections.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 38020, an unidentified off-highway motor vehicle subject to identification may be left standing upon a highway or public or private property adjacent to the place of business of a dealer of such motor vehicles when done so in connection with the loading and unloading or storage of such vehicles to be used in the dealer’s business, unless already prohibited by law.
